description: Upgrade commit graph visual feedback: remote/local HEAD indicators, branch labels in commit details, clearer branch attribution per commit.

userStories.title: Remote vs Local Branch Indicators in Commit Graph
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see where remote branches (origin/main) and local branches (main) are in the commit graph so I know what's pushed and what's local-only.
acceptanceCriteria: The commit graph already shows branch labels as pill badges via parseRefs in laneAssignment.ts
Remote branches (e.g., origin/main) are shown as a separate pill badge with a different color/style than local branches
Local branch pills use the current accent/branch color
Remote branch pills use a distinct muted/dimmed style (e.g., var(--text-muted) background with 'origin/' prefix visible)
If a local branch and its remote tracking branch point to the same commit, both pills are shown side by side
If they differ (local is ahead), only the local pill appears on the local commit and only the remote pill on the remote commit — making the divergence visible
HEAD indicator (the glow ring on the HEAD commit) remains unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The refs are already parsed in laneAssignment.ts parseRefs(). Remote refs have type 'remote' and name like 'origin/main'. The CommitGraph.tsx renders these as pill badges in the commit row. The change is mainly CSS — give remote ref pills a different style class.

userStories.title: Branch Labels in Commit Detail Panel
userStories.description: As a user, when I click a commit, I want the detail panel to show which branches contain that commit.
acceptanceCriteria: The commit detail panel (DetailPanel.tsx) shows a 'Branches' section below the refs/badges area
This section lists all branches (local and remote) that contain the selected commit
Use git branch --contains HASH and git branch -r --contains HASH to get the lists
Add a new IPC handler getBranchesContaining(repoPath, hash) in git-service.ts that runs both commands
Register in git-ipc.ts, expose in preload, add type declaration
Local branches shown with GitBranch icon, remote branches shown with Globe icon
The section is collapsible (like the files section) and starts expanded
If no branches contain the commit (orphaned), show 'No branches' message
Loading state while fetching (the git commands can be slow on large repos)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: git branch --contains HASH returns local branches. git branch -r --contains HASH returns remote branches. Run both, parse output (branch names, one per line, strip whitespace and * prefix for current branch). This is a new IPC endpoint following the existing pattern: git-service.ts -> git-ipc.ts -> preload -> type declaration.

userStories.title: Ahead/Behind Indicator for Current Branch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see how many commits my local branch is ahead of or behind its remote tracking branch.
acceptanceCriteria: In the commit graph header area or the status bar, show 'N ahead, M behind' for the current branch relative to its upstream
Use the existing git status or git rev-list --left-right --count to get ahead/behind counts
If ahead > 0: show a green up arrow with count (e.g., '↑3')
If behind > 0: show a red down arrow with count (e.g., '↓2')
If both: show both (e.g., '↑3 ↓2')
If up to date: show a green checkmark or 'Up to date'
If no upstream configured: show nothing or 'No upstream'
The indicator updates after fetch/pull/push operations
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The StatusBar already shows ahead/behind counts from the git status data. This story may already be partially implemented — check StatusBar.tsx. If it's already there, this story is about making it more prominent or adding it to the commit graph header. Use git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...@{upstream} for accurate counts.

userStories.title: Visual Distinction Between Branches in Graph Lanes
userStories.description: As a user, I want each branch in the commit graph to have a clearly distinct color so I can follow branch history visually.
acceptanceCriteria: The lane assignment algorithm already assigns colors per lane from LANE_COLORS in laneAssignment.ts
Verify that branch colors are sufficiently distinct — no two adjacent lanes should have similar colors
The current branch (HEAD) lane should always use var(--accent) color
Merge lines (bezier curves connecting branches) should use the color of the source branch
Branch pill badge colors should match their lane color (currently they may use a fixed color)
Add a subtle vertical lane background stripe (very low opacity, 2-3%) so users can follow a lane even when there are gaps between commits
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: laneAssignment.ts LANE_COLORS is a 10-color Catppuccin palette. The colors are assigned per lane and passed to the SVG renderer in CommitGraph.tsx via node.color. Pill badges currently use fixed CSS classes (refHead, refBranch, etc.) — they should use the lane color instead.

userStories.title: Commit Graph Row — Show Branch Name Per Commit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to quickly see which branch a commit belongs to without having to trace the lane lines.
acceptanceCriteria: Each commit row in the graph optionally shows a small branch indicator — the branch name that the commit is most directly associated with
This is NOT the full ref badges (those only appear on branch tips) — this is a subtle muted label showing the branch context
For commits on the current branch: no extra label needed (the lane color + HEAD indicator is enough)
For commits on other branches: show a small muted branch name label after the commit message (e.g., 'feature/foo' in var(--text-muted))
This information comes from the lane assignment — each commit is assigned to a lane which corresponds to a branch
The label should be truncated with ellipsis if too long (max 120px)
Can be toggled on/off via a setting or View menu option
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The lane assignment gives each commit a lane index and color, but doesn't directly track which branch name the lane represents. May need to enhance laneAssignment.ts to track the branch name per lane (the branch that opened the lane). Then pass it through to the commit row renderer.
